article_title: Star Catcher Raises $65 Million Series A to Beam Concentrated Sunlight at Satellites, Bringing Total Funding to $88 Million
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively sound space-funding coverage. Round mechanics verified verbatim: $65M oversubscribed Series A, $88M cumulative, $12.25M 2024 seed (Via Satellite), B Capital lead, Shield Capital and Cerberus Ventures co-leads, GreatPoint/Helena/Oceans/MVP existing investors, board seats for Raymond/Johnson/Rothzeid, Andrew Rush bio (Made in Space, Redwire). Technology claims verified: 10x more power, no retrofit, EverBank Stadium 300-foot March 2025 ground test, 1.1 kW Kennedy Space Center result, seven PPAs, $3B pipeline. Customer names verified: Starcloud, Loft Orbital, Astro Digital (Space.com). World-record-for-optical-power-beaming claim verified in PR Newswire press release. All major quotes (Rush 'orbital infrastructure', Johnson 'exploding demand', Raymond 'energy and infrastructure resilience') confirmed verbatim. One source-attribution slip: the 'free-flying satellite' beam target is attributed to SpaceDaily but is actually from Space.com (Rush quote, source-1). Fact is correct, cite is wrong. Filed correction.

source-0.status: Primary publication (Rule 9): PR Newswire press release. Verifies $65M, oversubscribed, B Capital lead, Shield+Cerberus co-leads, all named existing investors, board seats, Rush quote, 'no retrofit or custom receiver', 10x more power, $3B pipeline, seven PPAs, world record for optical power beaming, Johnson and Raymond quotes.

source-1.status: Space.com. Verifies EverBank Stadium March 2025 300-foot demo, Jacksonville, customer list (Starcloud/Loft Orbital/Astro Digital), AND the 'free-flying satellite' beam target attributed to Rush. The article misattributes that last specific to SpaceDaily.

source-2.status: Via Satellite. Verifies $12.25M 2024 seed round, Andrew Rush's Made in Space / Redwire background, CEO/co-founder framing.

source-3.status: SpaceDaily. Verifies 1.1 kW Kennedy Space Center result, 'first space-based optical power-beaming demonstration is planned for later this year. A second orbital mission is already in development', customer mix. Does NOT contain the 'free-flying satellite' phrase — that came from Space.com. Article cite needs correction.

source-4.status: POWER Magazine. Reputable energy-trade outlet, used for context.

source-5.status: Star Catcher's own news page (primary corporate source). Mirrors the PR Newswire release.

editor_notes.content_quality: News-category (~862 words), well-structured (Overview / Round / Technology / Customers / What We Don't Know). Neutral tone, no pumping language. Investor quotes balanced with skeptical 'What We Don't Know' section that flags peak-vs-average power, link budget, and valuation gaps.
editor_notes.originality: First Machine Herald coverage of the Star Catcher Series A. The April 2026 Starcloud article is referenced as related customer context (internal link), not duplicated coverage.
concerns: Cite misattribution: 'free-flying satellite' beam target attributed to SpaceDaily but actually a Rush quote in Space.com (source-1). Filed correction.
recommendations: Add satellitetoday.com, spacedaily.com, powermag.com, star-catcher.com to config/source_allowlist.txt — Star Catcher is the primary company domain.

content_preview.title: Star Catcher Raises $65 Million Series A to Beam Concentrated Sunlight at Satellites, Bringing Total Funding to $88 Million
content_preview.summary: The Florida space-energy startup, founded less than two years ago, will use the oversubscribed round led by B Capital to fly the first-ever space-based optical power-beaming demonstration later this year.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Florida-based space-energy startup Star Catcher said on May 12 that it has closed an oversubscribed $65 million Series A funding round to build what it calls the first power grid in space, [according to the company's announcement](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/star-catcher-raises-65-million-to-build-the-first-power-grid-in-space-302769128.html). The round brings Star Catcher's total capital raised to $88 million and is meant to bankroll the first-ever space-based optical ...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
